primary_rejection_reason: The integrated proposal fails to address my core, unresolvable concern: once regulators are alerted, the drug could be withdrawn or restricted before any careful, proportionate reassessment of the purported subgroup risk—directly harming the several hundred thousand patients currently benefiting.

The integrated proposal’s immediate external reporting directly contradicts my core belief that disclosure will likely trigger a regulatory review and market withdrawal, thereby harming several hundred thousand current patients who benefit from the drug.
Full, unredacted disclosure to regulators still gives no assurance the drug won’t be rapidly withdrawn or suspended, leaving current patients without timely access.

I can’t accept a policy that “primarily” protects occupants, because in the very rare unavoidable-harm cases where choices are constrained, that priority can still systematically shift collisions toward pedestrians/cyclists even if risk-spreading constraints exist.
